title: The Mines and Minerals Development (Amendment) Act, 2022

sections.heading: 1) This Act may be cited as the Mines and Minerals Short title and
sections.text:
1. (1) This Act may be cited as the Mines and Minerals Short title and
commence-
Development (Amendment) Act, 2022, and shall be read as one
ment
with the Mines and Minerals Development Act, 2015, in this Act Act No. 11
referred to as the principal Act. of 2015
(2) This Act shall come into operation on 1st January, 2023.

sections.heading: Section 89 of the principal Act is amended by the deletion Amendment
sections.text:
2. Section 89 of the principal Act is amended by the deletion Amendment
of subsection (2) and the substitution therefor of the following: of Section
89
(2) Where the base metal produced or recoverable under
the licence is copper, the mineral royalty payable shall be
applied at an incremental value in each price range at the
rate of —
(a) four percent of the norm value when the norm price
of copper is less than four thousand United States
dollars per tonne;
(b) six point five percent of the norm value when the
norm price of copper is four thousand United
States dollars or higher per tonne but less than
Single copies of this Act may be obtained from the Government Printer,

562 No. 29 of 2022] The Mines and Minerals Development
(Amendment)
five thousand United States dollars per tonne;
(c) eight point five percent of the norm value when the
norm price of copper is five thousand United
States dollars or higher per tonne but less than
seven thousand United States dollars per tonne;
and
(d) ten percent of the norm value when the norm price
of copper is seven thousand United States dollars
or higher per tonne.
